A brand new AI agent just dropped and it does something nobody has done before. You design your app in Figma. The AI reads the whole design, every button, every layout, every color, and then it builds the entire app for you. Real code, real front end, ready to ship. No dev team, no back and forth, no weeks of waiting. This is called design first vibe coding. And it is about to change how you build everything. Stay with me because I'm going to show you exactly how this works and how we're already using it to grow the AI profit boardroom. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of SEO agency Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every comment, so make sure you comment below. Today, we're breaking down a tool called Anything AI Agent. It's a design first vibe coding agent and it's one of the most exciting AI updates I've seen in a long time. We're going to cover what it is, how it works, why it matters, and I'll walk you through a real example of how you'd use it to build something for an actual business. So, stick around because this gets really good. Most AI coding tools today have a big flaw. They guess you type something like, "Build me a landing page." And they spit out something generic. It kind of looks okay, but it doesn't match your brand. It doesn't follow your layout. It ignores your design system completely. So then you spend hours fixing it, rewriting prompts, uh going back and forth trying to get it to look like what you actually imagined. Tools like Lovable or Visro are great, but they're starting from zero every time. They have no idea what your design looks like. They're making it up as they go. That's the core problem. The AI is building blind. And that's exactly what Anything AI agent is here to fix. Anything is an AI agent built for design first vibe coding. Here's the simple version. Instead of starting with a text prompt, you start with a design. You design your app in Figma first. Your layout, your sections, your buttons, your typography, all of it. You get it exactly how you want it to look. Then you import that design into anything. The agent reads the whole thing. It understands the structure. It maps every element. It sees the components, the spacing, the visual hierarchy for everything. And then it generates real working code. React Nex.js JS Tailwind production ready front end that actually matches what you designed. No guessing, no generic output. The AI builds from your real design, not from imagination. That's the breakthrough. That's what makes this different. Let me walk you through the workflow step by step so it's crystal clear. Step one, you design in Figma sheet. You build your UI like you normally would. Need buttons, hero sections, navigation, form night, whatever your project needs. You get it looking exactly right. Step two, you import into anything. You bring that design file into the agent. Step three, the AI converts it. The agent reads every element and maps it to code. A button in Figma becomes a React component. A stacked layout becomes flexbox. Your typography becomes Tailwind classes. It understands the whole visual system and translates it into clean code. Step four, you vibe code the features. This is where it gets really fun. Once the base is built, you prompt the agent to add functionality. things like add a login page, make this a full dashboard, add dark mode, build a weight list form into the hero. The AI edits the project based on your prompts. You're not writing code, you're directing it, like a creative director telling a developer what to do, except the developer is instant. That's design first vibe coding. And it works because the AI already understands your visual system before it starts building. Let me make this real. Say I want to build a brand new landing page for the AI profit boardroom. I open Figma and I design the whole thing. A strong hero section that explains what the community is about. Benefit blocks that show the value of AI automation. A section that explains who it's for. FAQ at the bottom. A clear CTA button that drives people to join. I get the design perfect, the colors, the layout, the spacing, all on brand, all exactly how I want it. Then I drop it into anything AI agent. The agent reads the design. It sees the hero. It sees the benefit blocks. It sees the CTA. It maps every element and it builds it. Clean code. Pixel perfect to my Figma file. Now I start prompting. Add a mobile responsive version of the hero. Done. Add a social proof section with community stats. Done. Add a sticky navigation bar at the top. Done. In under an hour, I've got a live working beautiful landing page for the AI profit boardroom. One that actually looks like my brand, not some AI generated template nobody asked for. Before tools like anything, designers and developers worked in completely separate lanes. The designer makes the Figma file, hands it to a developer. The developer spends a week building it. Something gets lost in translation. You go back and forth. Revisions take days. Now, that whole process gets compressed into hours. The designer creates the design. The AI builds the app. You're live the same day. For startup founders, solo entrepreneurs, small agencies, this is a massive unlock. You can move at a speed that was impossible before without a full team. And the quality is better, too. Because the AI is working from your actual design. The output matches your brand. It follows your design system. It doesn't look like every other AI built thing on the internet. That combination, speed and quality, is rare. Most tools give you one or the other. Design first AI coding is starting to give you both. Here's where AI development is heading and why this matters beyond just one tool. AI coding started with prompts. You type it builds. That was version one. We're now moving into version two. AI agents that read context first, then build. Anything reads your Figma design before it touches a single line of code. Other agents are starting to read your codebase, your brand guidelines, your existing architecture. The AI understands your system before it starts and that changes everything about the quality of the output. Version 3 is coming too. The AI doesn't just read your design. It creates the design for you. You describe an idea. It builds the Figma file. Then it builds the app. Then it deploys it. One idea, full product, minimal input needed. We're not fully there yet.
